core_mechanism: Global-in-time persistence of the boundary regularity of a sharp transported interface (a 'patch' — the discontinuity set of a piecewise-constant field advected by an incompressible flow), controlled through the regularity of the flow map generated by the velocity field
brief_reason: The abstract's central claim is that non-constant 'patch' initial data evolve while globally preserving C^{1,gamma}, W^{2,infinity}, and C^{2,gamma} boundary regularity of the evolved interface under advection — the classic transported-interface (vortex-patch-type) mechanism — with the large-parameter singular limit as the coupling coefficient tends to infinity a secondary reduction.
